code: result <- preprocess  dummy_setas <- function(list, mult) {   for (i in seq_along(list)) {     if (is.data.frame(list[[i]])) {           mult <- rep_len(mult, length.out = nrow(list[[i]]))           list[[i]][, ncol(list[[i]])] <- list[[i]][, ncol(list[[i]])] * mult     }   }   return(list) }  store_data <- list(result,                     dummy_setas(result, mult = c(1, 1.2)),                    dummy_setas(result, mult = c(2, 2.2)))  result <- combine_runs(outs = store_data, runs = c("run1", "run2", "run3"))

code: d <- system.file("extdata", "setas-model-new-trunk", package = "atlantistools")  nc_gen    <- file.path(d, "outputSETAS.nc") nc_prod   <- file.path(d, "outputSETASPROD.nc") dietcheck <- file.path(d, "outputSETASDietCheck.txt") yoy       <- file.path(d, "outputSETASYOY.txt") ssb       <- file.path(d, "outputSETASSSB.txt") prm_run   <- file.path(d, "VMPA_setas_run_fishing_F_Trunk.prm") prm_biol  <- file.path(d, "VMPA_setas_biol_fishing_Trunk.prm") fgs       <- file.path(d, "SETasGroupsDem_NoCep.csv") bgm       <- file.path(d, "VMPA_setas.bgm") init      <- file.path(d, "INIT_VMPA_Jan2015.nc")

code: # Read in raw untransformed data from nc_gen vars <- list("Nums", "StructN", "ResN", "N") grps <- list(groups_age, groups_age, groups_age, groups_rest) dfs_gen <- Map(load_nc, select_variable = vars, select_groups = grps,                MoreArgs = list(nc = nc_gen, bps = bps, fgs = fgs, prm_run = prm_run, bboxes = bboxes)) ## Reading in the nc file: /home/runner/work/_temp/Library/atlantistools/extdata/setas-model-new-trunk/outputSETAS.nc ## Reading in the nc file: /home/runner/work/_temp/Library/atlantistools/extdata/setas-model-new-trunk/outputSETAS.nc ## Reading in the nc file: /home/runner/work/_temp/Library/atlantistools/extdata/setas-model-new-trunk/outputSETAS.nc ## Reading in the nc file: /home/runner/work/_temp/Library/atlantistools/extdata/setas-model-new-trunk/outputSETAS.nc # Read in raw untransformed data from nc_prod vars <- list("Eat", "Grazing", "Growth") grps <- list(groups_age, groups_rest, groups_age) dfs_prod <- Map(load_nc, select_variable = vars, select_groups = grps,                 MoreArgs = list(nc = nc_prod, bps = bps, fgs = fgs, prm_run = prm_run, bboxes = bboxes)) ## Reading in the nc file: /home/runner/work/_temp/Library/atlantistools/extdata/setas-model-new-trunk/outputSETASPROD.nc ## Reading in the nc file: /home/runner/work/_temp/Library/atlantistools/extdata/setas-model-new-trunk/outputSETASPROD.nc ## Reading in the nc file: /home/runner/work/_temp/Library/atlantistools/extdata/setas-model-new-trunk/outputSETASPROD.nc # Read in physics flux <- load_nc_physics(nc = nc_gen, select_physics = c("eflux", "vflux"),                         prm_run = prm_run, bboxes = bboxes)  sink <- load_nc_physics(nc = nc_gen, select_physics = c("hdsource", "hdsink"),                         prm_run = prm_run, bboxes = bboxes)  physics <- load_nc_physics(nc = nc_gen,                             select_physics = c("salt", "NO3", "NH3", "Temp", "Chl_a", "Denitrifiction"),                            prm_run = prm_run, bboxes = bboxes)  # exclude sediment layer from salinity physics <- filter(physics, !(variable == "salt" & layer == max(layer) & time == min(time))) # exlucde water column from Denitrifiction physics <- filter(physics, !(variable == "Denitrifiction" & layer != max(layer) & time == min(time)))  vol_dz <- load_nc_physics(nc = nc_gen, select_physics = c("volume", "dz"),                           prm_run = prm_run, bboxes = bboxes)  dz <- dplyr::filter(vol_dz, variable == "dz") vol <- dplyr::filter(vol_dz, variable == "volume")  nominal_dz <- load_init(init = init, vars = "nominal_dz") %>%    as.data.frame() %>%    dplyr::filter(!is.na(layer))  # Read in Dietcheck df_dm <- load_dietcheck(dietcheck = dietcheck,                         fgs = fgs, prm_run = prm_run, convert_names = TRUE)  # Read in SSB/R ssb_rec <- load_rec(yoy = yoy, ssb = ssb, prm_biol = prm_biol)  # Read in misc   df_agemat <- prm_to_df(prm_biol = prm_biol, fgs = fgs, group = get_age_acronyms(fgs), parameter = "age_mat") dietmatrix <- load_dietmatrix(prm_biol, fgs, convert_names = TRUE)

title: model-preprocess
code: # Calculate biomass spatially bio_sp <- calculate_biomass_spatial(nums = dfs_gen[[1]], sn = dfs_gen[[2]], rn = dfs_gen[[3]], n = dfs_gen[[4]],                                     vol_dz = vol_dz, bio_conv = bio_conv, bps = bps)  # Aggregate spatial biomass to based on stanzas bio_sp_stanza <- combine_ages(bio_sp, grp_col = "species", agemat = df_agemat) ## Joining with `by = join_by(species)` # Aggregate biomass biomass <- bio_sp %>%   agg_data(groups = c("species", "time"), fun = sum)  biomass_age <- bio_sp %>%   filter(agecl > 2) %>%   agg_data(groups = c("species", "agecl", "time"), fun = sum)  # Aggregate Numbers! This is done seperately since numbers need to be summed! nums     <- agg_data(data = dfs_gen[[1]], groups = c("species", "time"), fun = sum) nums_age <- agg_data(data = dfs_gen[[1]], groups = c("species", "agecl", "time"), fun = sum) nums_box <- agg_data(data = dfs_gen[[1]], groups = c("species", "polygon", "time"), fun = sum)  # Aggregate the rest of the dataframes by mean! structn_age <- agg_data(data = dfs_gen[[2]],  groups = c("species", "time", "agecl"), fun = mean) resn_age    <- agg_data(data = dfs_gen[[3]],  groups = c("species", "time", "agecl"), fun = mean) eat_age     <- agg_data(data = dfs_prod[[1]], groups = c("species", "time", "agecl"), fun = mean) grazing     <- agg_data(data = dfs_prod[[2]], groups = c("species", "time"), fun = mean) growth_age  <- agg_data(data = dfs_prod[[3]], groups = c("species", "time", "agecl"), fun = mean)  # Calculate consumed biomass bio_cons <- calculate_consumed_biomass(eat = dfs_prod[[1]], grazing = dfs_prod[[2]], dm = df_dm,                                        vol = vol, bio_conv = bio_conv) %>%   agg_data(groups = c("pred", "agecl", "time", "prey"), fun = sum) ## 50% matching timesteps between PROD.nc and DietCheck.txt ## 11.61% data is lost due to missing diet data despite available eat data. ## 21.97% data is lost due to missing eat data despite available diet data. # Calculate spatial overlap sp_overlap <- calculate_spatial_overlap(biomass_spatial = bio_sp, dietmatrix = dietmatrix, agemat = df_agemat)  # Growth relative to initial conditions rec_weight <- prm_to_df(prm_biol = prm_biol, fgs = fgs,                          group = get_age_acronyms(fgs = fgs),                          parameter = c("KWRR", "KWSR", "AgeClassSize"))  pd <- load_init_weight(init = init, fgs = fgs, bboxes = bboxes) %>%   left_join(rec_weight) %>%   split(.$species) ## Joining with `by = join_by(species)` # Calculate weight difference from one ageclass to the next! for (i in seq_along(pd)) {   pd[[i]]$wdiff <- c((pd[[i]]$rn[1] + pd[[i]]$sn[1]) - (pd[[i]]$kwrr[1] + pd[[i]]$kwsr[1]),                      diff(pd[[i]]$rn + pd[[i]]$sn)) } pd <- do.call(rbind, pd) pd$growth_req <- pd$wdiff / (365 * pd$ageclasssize) if (any(pd$growth_req < 0)) {   warning("Required growth negative for some groups. Please check your initial conditions files.") }  gr_req <- pd %>%   select(species, agecl, growth_req)  gr_rel_init <- growth_age %>%   left_join(gr_req) %>%   mutate(gr_rel = (atoutput - growth_req) / growth_req) ## Joining with `by = join_by(species, agecl)` # Aggregate volume vertically. vol_ts <- agg_data(vol, groups = c("time", "polygon"), fun = sum, out = "volume")

text: first step atlantistools extraction data atlantis simulation. Data transformed following ‘tidy-dataframe’ framework Hadley Wickham. Output various variables (individual weight, physics, consumption…) standardised uniform format. simplify subsequent analysis following transformations applied: - Simulation time standardised time years. - Group names converted according colum ‘LongName’ functional groups file. - Data boundary boxes islands removed. - Data non existent layers removed. - Values stored column ‘atoutput’.

text: Compare model outoput observed data.
code: # Aggregate spatial biomass! biomass <- df_bio_spatial %>%   agg_data(groups = c("species", "time"), fun = sum)  plot_line(biomass, ncol = 3) plot_line(biomass, col = "species", ncol = 3) # Aggregate spatial biomass for fully age structured groups! biomass_age <- df_bio_spatial %>%   filter(agecl > 2) %>%   agg_data(groups = c("species", "agecl", "time"), fun = sum)  plot_line(biomass_age, col = "agecl") plot_line(biomass_age, wrap = "agecl", col = "species", ncol = 3) ex_data <- read.csv(file.path(system.file("extdata", "setas-model-new-becdev", package = "atlantistools"),                                "setas-bench.csv"), stringsAsFactors = FALSE) names(ex_data)[names(ex_data) == "biomass"] <- "atoutput"  data <- biomass data$model <- "atlantis" comp <- rbind(ex_data, data, stringsAsFactors = FALSE)  # Show atlantis as first factor! comp$model <- factor(comp$model, levels = c("atlantis", sort(unique(comp$model))[sort(unique(comp$model)) != "atlantis"]))  # Create plot plot_line(comp, col = "model", ncol = 3) # Use convert_relative_initial and {plot_add_box with plot_line. # Firstly, use convert_relative_initial to generate a relative time series first. # Aggregate the polygon and layer based data first. structn_age <- agg_data(data = structn,  groups = c("species", "time", "agecl"), fun = mean) df <- convert_relative_initial(structn_age)  # Create the base plot with plot_line. plot <- plot_line(df, col = "agecl")  # Add lower and upper range. plot_add_box(plot) # You can set the upper and lower range of the box as you like! plot_add_box(plot, range = c(0.8, 0.4)) # Create spatial timeseries plots in conjuction with custom_grid to plot physics data. plot <- plot_line(ref_physics, wrap = NULL) custom_grid(plot, grid_x = "polygon", grid_y = "variable") flux <- load_nc_physics(nc = nc_gen, select_physics = c("eflux", "vflux"),                         prm_run = prm_run, bboxes = bboxes, aggregate_layers = FALSE)  plot <- plot_line(flux, wrap = NULL, col = "variable") custom_grid(plot, grid_x = "polygon", grid_y = "layer") ## Warning: Removed 62 rows containing missing values or values outside the scale range ## (`geom_line()`).

text: Let’s say want change parameter biological parameter file. example recruit weights specific fish. case Small planktivorous fish (Code = FPS). can either change existing valu multiplication factor set new absolut value. case increase existing value factor 2. can use extract_prm check format resulting *.prm. However, checked function thoroughly normally set save_to_disc TRUE overwrite existing parameter file newly generated one Please make sure backup original files beforehand. can also pass vector groups change parameter multiple functional groups single call change_prm. helps automate tuning calibration model. addition useful tool make tuning reproduceable transparent modelers. case want change parameter values cohort structured groups multiple values per parameter (E.g. Clearance rate mum fish groups) please use change_prm_cohrt. Please note function works parameters whose values stored next row following flag *.prm file.

text: Calculate spatially explicit biomass time series group ageclass within model. Data read 'output[...].nc'. Biomass age-based groups calculated (StructN [mgN] + ResN [mgN]) * Numbers [individuals]. Biomass non age-based groups calculated N [mgN] * volume [m^3] (sediment-dz [m] / volume [m^3] epibenthic groups). mgN converted t based stettings biol.prm file. Simulation time steps converted time years based output timesteps given run.prm.

Using built in datasets. d <- system.file("extdata", "setas-model-new-trunk", package = "atlantistools") prm_biol <- file.path(d, "VMPA_setas_biol_fishing_Trunk.prm") fgs <- file.path(d, "SETasGroupsDem_NoCep.csv") init <- file.path(d, "INIT_VMPA_Jan2015.nc") bps <- load_bps(fgs = fgs, init = init)  bio_conv <- get_conv_mgnbiot(prm_biol = prm_biol)  df <- calculate_biomass_spatial(nums = ref_nums, sn = ref_structn, rn = ref_resn, n = ref_n,                                 vol_dz = ref_vol_dz, bio_conv = bio_conv, bps = bps)  # 2. Read in dataframes from existing Atlantis simulation. bboxes <- get_boundary(boxinfo = load_box(file.path(d, "VMPA_setas.bgm"))) nc_gen <- file.path(d, "outputSETAS.nc") prm_run <- file.path(d, "VMPA_setas_run_fishing_F_Trunk.prm")  groups_age <- c("Planktiv_S_Fish", "Pisciv_S_Fish") groups_rest <- c("Cephalopod", "Megazoobenthos", "Diatom", "Lab_Det", "Ref_Det")  nums <- load_nc(nc = nc_gen, bps = bps, fgs = fgs,                 select_groups = groups_age, select_variable = "Nums",                 prm_run = prm_run, bboxes = bboxes) #> Reading in the nc file: /home/runner/work/_temp/Library/atlantistools/extdata/setas-model-new-trunk/outputSETAS.nc sn <- load_nc(nc = nc_gen, bps = bps, fgs = fgs,               select_groups = groups_age, select_variable = "StructN",               prm_run = prm_run, bboxes = bboxes) #> Reading in the nc file: /home/runner/work/_temp/Library/atlantistools/extdata/setas-model-new-trunk/outputSETAS.nc rn <- load_nc(nc = nc_gen, bps = bps, fgs = fgs,               select_groups = groups_age, select_variable = "ResN",               prm_run = prm_run, bboxes = bboxes) #> Reading in the nc file: /home/runner/work/_temp/Library/atlantistools/extdata/setas-model-new-trunk/outputSETAS.nc n <- load_nc(nc = nc_gen, bps = bps, fgs = fgs,              select_groups = groups_rest, select_variable = "N",              prm_run = prm_run, bboxes = bboxes) #> Reading in the nc file: /home/runner/work/_temp/Library/atlantistools/extdata/setas-model-new-trunk/outputSETAS.nc vol <- load_nc_physics(nc = nc_gen, select_physics = c("volume", "dz"),                        prm_run = prm_run, bboxes = bboxes, aggregate_layers = FALSE)  df <- calculate_biomass_spatial(nums = nums, sn = sn, rn = rn, n = n, vol_dz = vol,                                 bio_conv = bio_conv, bps = bps)  # 3. Read in dataframes from existing Atlantis simulation with Map(). vars <- list("Nums", "StructN", "ResN", "N") grps <- list(groups_age, groups_age, groups_age, groups_rest) dfs <- Map(load_nc, select_variable = vars, select_groups = grps,            MoreArgs = list(nc = nc_gen, bps = bps, fgs = fgs,                            prm_run = prm_run, bboxes = bboxes)) #> Reading in the nc file: /home/runner/work/_temp/Library/atlantistools/extdata/setas-model-new-trunk/outputSETAS.nc #> Reading in the nc file: /home/runner/work/_temp/Library/atlantistools/extdata/setas-model-new-trunk/outputSETAS.nc #> Reading in the nc file: /home/runner/work/_temp/Library/atlantistools/extdata/setas-model-new-trunk/outputSETAS.nc #> Reading in the nc file: /home/runner/work/_temp/Library/atlantistools/extdata/setas-model-new-trunk/outputSETAS.nc  df <- calculate_biomass_spatial(nums = dfs[[1]], sn = dfs[[2]], rn = dfs[[3]], n = dfs[[4]],                                 vol_dz = vol, bio_conv = bio_conv, bps = bps)

what: Calculate the consumed biomass in [t] of prey j by predator i. — calculate_consumed_biomass
title: Calculate the consumed biomass in [t] of prey j by predator i. — calculate_consumed_biomass
text: Consumption data extracted output[...]PROD.nc. Age based groups stored "Eat_" non age based groups "Grazing_". Units mg N m^-3 d^-1. Factors species, time, box agecl (present). refer species pred indicate predator perspective. Diet contribution data extracted DietCheck.txt. Currently works models based trunk code. Units  consumed biomass calculated follows: - Calculate consumed biomass Eat (Grazing) * boxvolume per time, pred, agecl, box. - Convert biomass [t]. - Combine diet contributions calculate consumed biomass prey species.

title: Change the availability matrix to simplify automated ATLANTIS calibrations. — change_avail
text: dietmatrix Dataframe 'long' format containing information availabilities columns 'pred', 'prey', 'pred_stanza', 'prey_stanza', 'code', 'prey_id' 'avail'. dataframe generated load_dietmatrix(). pred Character vector predator Acronyms (see get_acronyms()). Selecting NULL pred results predators selected. can helpful want increase feeding pressure specific prey item groups. Default NULL. pred_stanza Integer vector indicating predator juvenile (= 1) adult (= 2). pred pred_stanza need length. rare instances, e.g. pred_stanza NULL one single integer either pred_stanzas selected single pred_stanza applied predators. Default NULL. prey List character vectors prey Acronyms (see get_acronyms()). pred prey need length. Selecting NULL prey results prey groups selected. can helpful want increase available prey specific predator overall. Default NULL. roc Vector multiplication factors shall applied old set parameters. Please supply one value per selected group. case relative FALSE new absolute values can passed roc. relative Logical TRUE values changed relative base values. FALSE new values can passed directly. Default NULL. consecutive Boolean indicating multiple calls change_avail performed one another TRUE. Default FALSE.

text: prm_biol Character string giving connection biological parameterfile. filename usually contains biol_fishing end .prm. select_acronyms Character vector functional groups shall read . Names match ones used *.prm file. Check column "Code" "functionalGroups.csv" clarification. roc Vector multiplication factors shall applied old set parameters. Please supply one value per selected group. case relative FALSE new absolute values can passed roc. parameter Character value model parameter shall changed. one parameter can selected per function call. relative Logical TRUE values changed relative base values. FALSE new values can passed directly. Default TRUE. save_to_disc Logical indicating resulting prm file overwritten (TRUE) (FALSE). Defaults TRUE. version_flag version ATLANTIS model. 1 bec_dev, 2 trunk. default 2..

title: Change biological parameterfile for parameters which expect multiple values. — change_prm_cohort
text: prm_biol Character string giving connection biological parameterfile. filename usually contains biol_fishing end .prm. select_acronyms Character vector functional groups shall read . Names match ones used *.prm file. Check column "Code" "functionalGroups.csv" clarification. roc Matrix multiplication factors shall applied old set parameters. Please supply one row per selected group. row many entries parameter . E.g. want change clearance rate two fish groups need supply matrix 2 rows 10 columns. case use different cohort numbers age-structured groups supply list multiplication factors. list entry group specific. parameter Character value model parameter shall changed. one parameter can selected per function call. relative Logical TRUE values changed relative base values. FALSE new values can passed directly. Default TRUE. save_to_disc Logical indicating resulting prm file overwritten (TRUE) (FALSE). Defaults TRUE.

code: d <- system.file("extdata", "setas-model-new-trunk", package = "atlantistools")  new_prm <- change_prm_cohort(prm_biol = file.path(d, "VMPA_setas_biol_fishing_Trunk.prm"),                              select_acronyms = c("FPS", "FVS"),                              roc = matrix(rep(2, times = 20), nrow = 2, ncol = 10),                              parameter = "C",                              save_to_disc = FALSE) # C_FPS is in line 640. Old values are 0.0002 0.3 0.6 0.6 0.6 0.6 0.5 0.5 0.4 and 0.4. new_prm[640:641] #> [1] "C_FPS            10"                            #> [2] "4e-04\t0.6\t1.2\t1.2\t1.2\t1.2\t1\t1\t0.8\t0.8" # C_FVS is in line 652. Old values are 40.0 40.0 40.0 120.0 150.0 250.0 250.0 300.0 300.0 and 300.0. new_prm[652:653] #> [1] "C_FVS            10"                           #> [2] "80\t80\t80\t240\t300\t500\t500\t600\t600\t600"  # Also works for lists as argument new_prm <- change_prm_cohort(prm_biol = file.path(d, "VMPA_setas_biol_fishing_Trunk.prm"),                              select_acronyms = c("FPS", "FVS"),                              roc = list(rep(3, times = 10), rep(2, times = 10)),                              parameter = "C",                              save_to_disc = FALSE)

text: mortFile Character string giving path Mort.txt file. filename usually contains Mort ends .txt". prm_run Character string giving connection run parameterfile. filename usually contains run_fishing ends .prm". fgs Character string giving connection functional groups file. filename usually contains Groups end .csv. convert_names Logical indicating group codes transformed LongNames (TRUE) (default = FALSE).

code: d <- system.file("extdata", "setas-model-new-becdev", package = "atlantistools") mortFile <- file.path(d, "outputSETASMort.txt") prm_run <- file.path(d, "VMPA_setas_run_fishing_F_New.prm") fgs <- file.path(d, "SETasGroups.csv")  df <- load_mort(mortFile, prm_run, fgs) head(df) #> # A tibble: 6 × 4 #>    time code  source atoutput #>   <dbl> <chr> <chr>     <dbl> #> 1     1 BB    F             0 #> 2     2 BB    F             0 #> 3     3 BB    F             0 #> 4     4 BB    F             0 #> 5     5 BB    F             0 #> 6     6 BB    F             0  d <- system.file("extdata", "setas-model-new-trunk", package = "atlantistools") mortFile <- file.path(d, "outputSETASMort.txt") prm_run <- file.path(d, "VMPA_setas_run_fishing_F_Trunk.prm") fgs <- file.path(d, "SETasGroupsDem_NoCep.csv")  df <- load_mort(mortFile, prm_run, fgs) head(df) #> # A tibble: 6 × 4 #>    time code  source atoutput #>   <dbl> <chr> <chr>     <dbl> #> 1     1 BB    F             0 #> 2     2 BB    F             0 #> 3     3 BB    F             0 #> 4     4 BB    F             0 #> 5     5 BB    F             0 #> 6     6 BB    F             0

title: Load Atlantis outputfiles (netcdf) — load_nc
text: nc Character string giving connection netcdf file read . filename usually contains output ends .nc". fgs Character string giving connection functional groups file. filename usually contains Groups end .csv. bps Vector character strings giving complete list epibenthic functional groups (present sediment layer). names match column 'Name' 'functionalGroups.csv' file. Can created load_bps.#' select_groups Character vector funtional groups shall read . Names match ones used netcdf file. Check column "Name" "functionalGroups.csv" clarification. select_variable Character value specifying variable load. one variable options available (.e., c( "N", "Nums", "ResN", "StructN", "Eat", "Growth", "Prodn", "Grazing") ) can loaded time. prm_run Character string giving connection run parameterfile. filename usually contains run_fishing ends .prm". bboxes Integer vector giving box-id boundary boxes. Can created get_boundary. check_acronyms Logical testing functional-groups select_groups inactive current model run. omitted output. warn_zeros Logical indicating check actual zeros data shall printed . Default FALSE. report Logical indicating progress bars shall printed (TRUE) (FALSE). Default TRUE.

title: Load Atlantis outputfiles (netcdf) — load_nc
text: data.frame long format following column names:   species, timestep, polygon, agecl, atoutput (.e., variable).

title: Load Atlantis outputfiles (netcdf) — load_nc
code: d <- system.file("extdata", "setas-model-new-trunk", package = "atlantistools")  nc <- file.path(d, "outputSETAS.nc") fgs <- file.path(d, "SETasGroupsDem_NoCep.csv") bps <- load_bps(init = file.path(d, "INIT_VMPA_Jan2015.nc"), fgs = fgs) bboxes <- get_boundary(boxinfo = load_box(bgm = file.path(d, "VMPA_setas.bgm"))) prm_run <- file.path(d, "VMPA_setas_run_fishing_F_Trunk.prm")  test <- load_nc(nc = nc, bps = bps, fgs = fgs, prm_run = prm_run, bboxes = bboxes,   select_groups = c("Planktiv_S_Fish", "Cephalopod", "Diatom"),   select_variable = "ResN") #> Reading in the nc file: /home/runner/work/_temp/Library/atlantistools/extdata/setas-model-new-trunk/outputSETAS.nc  test <- load_nc(nc = nc, bps = bps, fgs = fgs, prm_run = prm_run, bboxes = bboxes,   select_groups = c("Planktiv_S_Fish", "Cephalopod", "Diatom"),   select_variable = "Nums") #> Reading in the nc file: /home/runner/work/_temp/Library/atlantistools/extdata/setas-model-new-trunk/outputSETAS.nc

title: atlantistools 0.3.2.9000
text: calculate_biomass_spatial Calculate total biomass tonnes every group ageclass combination per time, polygon layer. Data read ‘output[…].nc’ using load_nc function. Biomass age based groups calculated (StructN [mgN] + ResN [mgN]) * Numbers [individuals]. Biomass non age based groups calculated N [mgN] * volume [m^3] (sediment-dz [m] / volume [m^3] epibenthic groups). mgN converted tonnes based stettings biol.prm file. Simulation time steps converted time years based output timesteps given run.prm. Group names unified match column ‘LongName’ functionalGroups.csv. transformations applied every calculate load function guarantee structure dataframes. calculate_consumed_biomass Calculate consumed biomass tonnes every preygroup predator/predatorageclass combination timestep polygon. Consumption data extracted output[…]PROD.nc. Age based groups stored “Eat_” non age based groups “Grazing_”. Units consumed mass mg N m^-3 d^-1. consumed biomass scaled boxvolume transformed tonnes. Diet contribution data extracted DietCheck.txt. Currently works models based trunk code. Units % diet contribution. calculate consumed biomass preytype total consumed biomass (per predator/ageclass/time/polygon) multiplied dietcontribution prey predator. calculate_spatial_overlap spatial overlap predator prey groups calculated based concept Schoener’s similarity index ranging 1 (perfect overlap) 0 (zero overlap). function returns 2 dataframes. Firstly Schoener index per predator/ageclass/prey combination. Secondly index calculated weighted mean per predator/predatorageclass prey availabilities weights. second version can used test overlap predator emphasis important prey groups. current version potential polygons layers (islands/boundary boxes non-existent layers excluded) used calculate spatial overlap even predator never present specific polygon layer. may result low overlap indices case species narrow spatial distribution. check_growth helps track changes group- age specific individual weight time. can either track maximum relative change individual weight whole simulation period relative change within year case multiple outputs per year. allow easily track unrealistically high changes individual weight throughout simulation. plot_diet updated way plot_diet works. new version plot_diet works trunk models. Please use plot_diet_bec_dev models based bec_dev code version. plot_diet() now displays realised diet contribution (per predator prey perspective) based amount consumed biomas. Please note, plot_diet_bec_dev work DietCheck.txt anymore. Please use SpecPredMort.txt instead. noted resulting plot gives indication realisd diets bec_dev models. sc_init sanity check initial conditions file. Given prey densities, C MUM values initial conditions file required growth per group ageclass (growth needed grow ageclass ageclass +1) compared realised growth given initial conditions file. plot_sc_init function tightly linked sc_init.
